mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions through lower discount rates and tighter credit spreads, lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive in more than 55% of periods when FEDFUNDS has declined for 2 consecutive monthly observations, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration, weaker household income growth, and higher recession probability, which should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average forward return is nonnegative over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS relative to recent months signals stronger nominal consumption and revenue growth, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward S&P 500 returns fail to exceed the unconditional mean following above-trend RSAFS growth, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO is rising while CPIAUCSL remains subdued, output growth improves without forcing tighter policy, which supports margins and equity multiples and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO-up and CPIAUCSL-slow conditions do not outperform either signal alone, or if triggered 20-day returns are not positive in at least 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A housing downturn defined by HOUST breaking below its recent level reduces construction activity, household wealth effects, and credit demand, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average by at least 8%
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns remain positive in more than 60% of housing-breakdown episodes or if the average return is positive across two consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: Joint payroll growth and low unemployment identify a durable expansion in labor income and consumer spending, which supports earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-UNRATE joint condition does not beat PAYEMS alone in out-of-sample rank IC, or if 60-day returns are not positive in at least 58% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: In a monetary tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate with stable-to-elevated CPIAUCSL compresses valuation multiples and lifts recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises at least 0.25pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if tightening episodes do not generate below-baseline 20-day returns, or if positive returns exceed 50% of triggered observations after controlling for CPIAUCSL.

mechanism_narrative: A broad demand slowdown is present when RSAFS weakens while GDPC1 growth also decelerates, signaling fading nominal spending and lower earnings momentum that should weaken S&P 500 returns over the next 60 days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined RSAFS-GDPC1 slowdown signal does not produce negative average 60-day returns, or if its hit rate is not at least 5 percentage points below the unconditional baseline.
